title: Loop A: escalate_human outcome when DA-retry budget exhausts with a live high-severity blocker
source: loop-a-tick #13 (2026-05-26): DA explicitly argued that exhausting MAX_DA_RETRIES while >=1 high-severity objection persists should escalate to a human, not silently record da_blocked.
why: When run_preflight hits da_retry_count == MAX_DA_RETRIES with a BLOCK that still carries high-severity objections, the tick is recorded as terminal 'da_blocked' indistinguishably from a low/medium-only block. But an unresolved HIGH after the budget is spent is a signal the loop could not self-correct -- exactly the case a human (or the daily skeptic) should see. Folding it into the same da_blocked bucket hides the strongest candidates-that-failed-for-process-reasons from the daily triage.
value_rationale: Medium: improves the daily-report signal and routes genuinely-stuck lineages to humans instead of burying them. Lower urgency than B0043/B0044 since the tick still terminates safely today; this is about observability, not correctness.
recommendation: In run_preflight, when the budget is exhausted AND da_objections_high >= 1, record a distinct outcome (e.g. 'da_blocked_escalate_human') and surface it prominently in results/loop_a/<date>.md so the daily skeptic/human picks it up. Plain low/medium-only exhaustion stays 'da_blocked'. Note interaction with B0043: once the DA can see the dossier, fewer lineages will exhaust on unfixable dossier-blind blockers, so this should fire rarely and meaningfully.
out_of_scope: Not adding human-in-the-loop tooling beyond the report flag; not auto-promoting escalated items.

history.reason: fix(B0043): thread regime_stats_dossier into the devil's-advocate payload
